summary: Parallel review: FAIL (2 critical, 5 warning, 7 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/parsers.mjs:27` — `parseJUnit` ignores `<error>` elements (only parses `<failure>`). JUnit XML spec defines `<error>` for unexpected exceptions. This creates a false-PASS risk for Java-style test output. Add `<error>` handling.

🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:31` — Exit-code fallback sets `findings.critical: 1` when no structured output is detected but exit != 0, without writing `validator-findings.json`. Claims findings without evidence artifact. Consider using 0 for critical count in this path.
🟡 `bin/lib/gate.mjs:40` — `writeGateArtifacts` does not validate `taskId` internally; add a path-traversal guard at the point of use for defense-in-depth, in case future callers omit the check
🔴 bin/lib/parsers.mjs:273 — `isGenericJson` is dead code: exported function with zero consumers (production or test). `detectAndParse` inlines JSON detection at L298-304. Delete the function.
🔴 bin/lib/gate.mjs:160 — `const verdict = exitCode === 0 ? "PASS" : "FAIL"` is dead: declared but never read. All downstream code uses `finalVerdict` from `processGateOutput`. Delete the line.
🟡 bin/lib/parsers.mjs:250-268 — `isJUnitXml`, `isTAP`, `isProblemMatcher` are exported but only used internally by `detectAndParse`. Remove `export` keyword.
🟡 bin/lib/gate.mjs:86-89 — Path-traversal regex duplicated identically at run.mjs:56-57. Extract shared helper.
